# Validate archive migration and document anomaly hooks

## Context
This is bucket B5 of Epic #11187 (Phase 5).
Final validation and documentation for the single-root archive migration to ensure long-term stability and prevent regressions.

## The Problem
Missing documentation leads to regression. Anomaly detection is needed if `closedAt` shifts occur to prevent data from being placed in the wrong archive chunk.

## The Architectural Reality
- `learn/agentos/sandman-handoff-format.md`
- `learn/agentos/GitHubWorkflow.md`

## The Fix
Implement closedAt-shift anomaly hooks for human review at dry-run. Document the new lazy chunking algorithm and sealed-chunk semantics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Anomaly detection for closedAt shifts is active.
- [ ] Documentation updated in `GitHubWorkflow.md` and `sandman-handoff-format.md`.

## Out of Scope
- Code changes outside of anomaly detection hooks.

## Related
- Epic #11187

Origin Session ID: 2c4aa4df-2628-45ae-a9c2-156fd9308f21

- 2026-05-13T12:59:51Z @tobiu referenced in commit `8c70b29` - "feat(sync): implement archive anomaly hook for bucket shifts (#11288) (#11298)

- Added oldVersion extraction logic to `IssueSyncer.#planArchiveBuckets` using the existing metadata path.
- Implemented anomaly-detection hook that logs a warning when a closedAt/version shift causes an issue to relocate across archive buckets.
- Fixed scope of `aiConfig` in `IssueSyncer.spec.mjs` to resolve unit test failures.
- Added regression coverage for the anomaly hook to prevent silent metadata bucket shifts.

Co-authored-by: neo-gemini-pro <neo-gemini-pro@users.noreply.github.com>"
- 2026-05-14T11:36:30Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #11187
- 2026-05-14T15:05:48Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `fae8dd6` - "test(github-workflow): supersede #11288 unversioned-target scenario in IssueSyncer spec (#11360)

#11360's syncer fix removes the `'unversioned'` archive fallback (architectural bug
fix per Epic #11187 Phase 6 mental model: closed-post-latest-release items stay in
active, not pre-staged into not-yet-existing version archives).

The pre-existing IssueSyncer.spec.mjs #11288 anomaly-hook test asserted that:
  1. A bucket-shift to `'unversioned'` fires an `[ARCHIVE ANOMALY]` warning
  2. The final path contains `'unversioned'`

Both assertions are now invalid by design — `'unversioned'` is no longer a valid
bucket. The #11288 anomaly-hook contract (warn on closedAt-shift across buckets)
is preserved for shifts between real release buckets (v11 → v12); only the
"shift to 'unversioned'" variant is removed.

Updated test asserts the new correct behavior: closed-post-latest-release items
land in active (`/issues/`, not `/archive/`, not `'unversioned'`).

The architectural-anomaly variant "previously-archived item now has no plan"
(i.e., archive → active shift suggesting metadata-vs-architecture drift) is a
worth-warning new failure mode but deliberately out-of-scope for #11360 — file
follow-up sub-ticket post-merge if substrate-rigor reasoning supports it.

Refs #11288 #11360 #11187"
- 2026-05-14T15:19:48Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11362
- 2026-05-14T15:40:53Z @tobiu referenced in commit `559c73d` - "fix(github-workflow): substrate cleanup — legacy archives + 195-PR collision + syncer fallback + pr- prefix (#11360) (#11362)

* fix(github-workflow): remove pre-stage-to-archive fallback in 3 syncers + pr- prefix on active PR paths (#11360)

Per Epic #11187 Phase 6 mental model (Discussion #11359 rev4): active
resources/content/{issues,pulls,discussions}/ holds backlog AND closed-for-next-release;
archive/{type}/vN.M.K/ holds past-release items only, created at release-cut by publish.mjs.

Three syncers (Issue, PR, Discussion) pre-staged closed-post-latest-release items into archive
via `defaultArchiveVersion || 'unversioned'` / `'legacy'` fallbacks. Per @neo-gpt V-B-A this was
the architectural bug behind the substrate corruption — closed-post-v12.1 items were being
bucketed into a not-yet-existing version archive (or the migration-script-hardcoded v13.0.0).

Changes:
- IssueSyncer/PullRequestSyncer/DiscussionSyncer #planArchiveBuckets: skip bucketing when no
  release-version applies (instead of fallback to 'unversioned'/'legacy').
- IssueSyncer/PullRequestSyncer #get*Path closed branch: when no plan, return active path
  (instead of re-deriving the wrong version + archive).
- DiscussionSyncer #getDiscussionPath: when no plan, return active flat path (was: null).
- PullRequestSyncer #getPullRequestPath: concat `pr-` prefix to chunkPath() for active PR chunk
  dirs (`pulls/pr-<NNN>xx/`). Previously produced wrong dir `pulls/<NNN>xx/`. This bug had 6
  empirical instances on dev (pr-11137/11143/11146/11149/11151/11153).

Adds ai/scripts/migrate-substrate-11360.mjs — one-shot manifest generator + applier for the
data-corpus cleanup. Sibling pattern of migrate-pr-archive-ac8.mjs (which this PR deletes in the
subsequent commit). Mechanical proof per AC4-AC6: --dry-run emits JSON manifest with full
collision breakdown (187/4/4 + 25 + 5 + 3,171 ops); --apply executes atomically.

Code-fix commit. Data-corpus cleanup follows in the next commit (#11360 AC7 atomicity).

Refs #11187 #11360 #11359

* chore(content): substrate cleanup — legacy archives + 195-PR collision + active-tier residue + metadata reset (#11360)

Atomic per AC7: file cleanup + .sync-metadata.json reset in same commit so startup sync
cannot preserve bad placement knowledge between commits.

Generated via `node ai/scripts/migrate-substrate-11360.mjs --apply` (committed in prior commit).
Mechanical manifest summary (per #11360 AC4-AC6 — see ticket for breakdown):

- 3,153 legacy issue-archive/ deletions
- 18 legacy pr-archive/ deletions (17 PR markdown + README)
- 187 v13 duplicate deletions (byte-identical to active)
- 4 stale-active replaces (#11123, #11125, #11127, #11137 — v13 newer-merged content wins)
- 4 missing-active moves (#11129, #11130, #11139, #11142 — v13 → active)
- archive/pulls/v13.0.0/ subtree deletion
- 25 flat issues moved to chunked: issues/issue-N.md → issues/<NNN>xx/issue-N.md
- 5 wrong-dir pulls moved: pulls/111xx/pr-N.md → pulls/pr-111xx/pr-N.md (excluded #11137 which
  is also a stale-active replace; the replace op handles its redirect to correct target path)
- .sync-metadata.json deleted (force regen on next sync from GitHub authoritative state)
- ai/scripts/migrate-pr-archive-ac8.mjs deleted (one-shot, harmful --fallback-version v13.0.0
  default was the bug enabler per Discussion #11359)

Cross-family signals on the graduating Discussion #11359 rev4:
- @neo-opus-ada (author): [GRADUATION_PROPOSED]
- @neo-gemini-pro: [GRADUATION_APPROVED] (rev1 + §6.3 pragmatic-extension to rev4)
- @neo-gpt: [GRADUATION_APPROVED] rev4 (after DEFERRED at rev3; V-B-A surfaced this exact
  collision-aware 187/4/4 breakdown which is now mechanically proven by the manifest)

Refs #11187 #11360 #11359

* chore(test): remove orphaned migratePrArchiveAc8.spec — script deleted in prior commit (#11360)

The migrate-pr-archive-ac8.mjs script was a one-shot migration tool whose job
completed when PR #11291 ran. Its --fallback-version v13.0.0 default became
the bug-enabler that #11360 fixes. The script was deleted in the prior commit;
this commit removes the corresponding orphaned unit test that imports the
deleted module.

Mechanical proof of the new migration (migrate-substrate-11360.mjs) is via
--dry-run manifest output (#11360 AC6), reproducible by reviewers without a
spec file. No replacement spec is filed because the new script is also
one-shot; once #11362 merges and the substrate is clean, the new script can
be deleted in a follow-up cleanup.

Refs #11360

* test(github-workflow): supersede #11288 unversioned-target scenario in IssueSyncer spec (#11360)

#11360's syncer fix removes the `'unversioned'` archive fallback (architectural bug
fix per Epic #11187 Phase 6 mental model: closed-post-latest-release items stay in
active, not pre-staged into not-yet-existing version archives).

The pre-existing IssueSyncer.spec.mjs #11288 anomaly-hook test asserted that:
  1. A bucket-shift to `'unversioned'` fires an `[ARCHIVE ANOMALY]` warning
  2. The final path contains `'unversioned'`

Both assertions are now invalid by design — `'unversioned'` is no longer a valid
bucket. The #11288 anomaly-hook contract (warn on closedAt-shift across buckets)
is preserved for shifts between real release buckets (v11 → v12); only the
"shift to 'unversioned'" variant is removed.

Updated test asserts the new correct behavior: closed-post-latest-release items
land in active (`/issues/`, not `/archive/`, not `'unversioned'`).

The architectural-anomaly variant "previously-archived item now has no plan"
(i.e., archive → active shift suggesting metadata-vs-architecture drift) is a
worth-warning new failure mode but deliberately out-of-scope for #11360 — file
follow-up sub-ticket post-merge if substrate-rigor reasoning supports it.

Refs #11288 #11360 #11187

* fix(github-workflow): address @neo-gpt PR #11362 Cycle 1 executable-surface blockers (#11360)

Four narrow fixes for blockers @neo-gpt surfaced in
https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11362#issuecomment-4451929148 (correction at
https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11362#pullrequestreview-4291032804):

1. IssueSyncer.spec.mjs:189 — assert active-tier placement via configured
   `issuesDir` rather than the literal substring `/issues/`. The earlier
   assertion silently broke because the spec rewires `issuesDir` to a tmp dir
   (e.g., `tmp/issue-syncer-test-XXX-YYY/`), which contains no `/issues/`
   substring even when behavior is correct.

2. package.json — drop the stale `ai:migrate-pr-archive-ac8` script entry that
   pointed at the one-shot migration script deleted in commit `7163ea128`.

3. resources/content/pulls/pr-111xx/pr-11127.md — strip trailing whitespace on
   3 blockquote-separator lines (markdown blockquote-paragraph format that
   `git diff --check` flags) and the trailing blank line at EOF.

4. ai/scripts/migrate-substrate-11360.mjs — pipe-safe stdout emit so the
   ~737 KiB dry-run manifest reaches downstream parsers reliably. Replaces
   `console.log(...); process.exit(0)` with
   `process.stdout.write(..., () => process.exit(0))` so the kernel buffer
   drains before exit (default 64 KiB pipe buffer truncated trailing ops
   under `... | head` / `... | jq`).

Locally: `IssueSyncer.spec.mjs` 4/4 pass; `PullRequestSyncer.spec.mjs` 1/1 pass;
`node ai/scripts/migrate-substrate-11360.mjs --dry-run | wc -c` = 737347;
`node ... --dry-run | python3 -c 'import json,sys; json.load(sys.stdin)'`
parses cleanly with `operations.length === 3399` matching the PR body.

Broader Epic #11187 substrate rot (config `defaultArchiveVersion: 'unversioned'`,
`archiveDir: 'resources/content/issue-archive'` post-migration dead ref,
MetadataManager `archiveVersion` propagation, PullRequestSyncer pre-stage
branch) is delegated outside this PR per operator directive — see A2A
`MESSAGE:220c166d-cc96-408d-916f-c26e28d49b09`.
